Instructions for creating and using header overrides in unit tests.

# Purpose
The document provides instructions for creating and using header overrides in unit tests. It explains how to set up an override tree with custom versions of header files that will take precedence over default and source-tree headers. The document specifies that the relative paths of the override headers must match those in the source tree to ensure correct shadowing. It also includes a guideline to avoid including function prototypes in override headers to prevent discrepancies between firmware and override headers, which could lead to incorrect test results. Instead, it suggests refactoring headers to separate function prototypes and inline function definitions, and to provide overrides only for the inline definitions.
